raw_text: Håller absolut med! Den första Fright Night var en upplevelse när det begav sig 1985. Chris Sarandon med ensemble är en riktig fullträff! Mästerligt regisserad av Tom Holland som även gjorde första Chucky-filmen - Child's Play, ett par år senare. Riktiga 80-talsklassiker!

Brukar du se film på dvd? Intresset för dvd-filmer och Blu-ray växer, och det senaste året har försäljningen tagit fart på flera håll.
Hos auktionssajten Tradera har försäljningen av fysisk film ökat med 25 procent på ett år. Och fem år efter att den sista stora kedjan slutade hyra ut dvd-film i Sverige har en ny hyrvideobutik öppnat i Malmö.
– Jag är så himla trött på streamingtjänster. Det kändes som att de försökte ta min tid, säger Francisco, som just har lämnat tillbaka två dvd:er. 
Hör mer i Kulturnytt: https://www.sverigesradio.se/.../dvd-filmen-pa-frammarsch...

![Archive Image](/assets/journal-images/facebook-assets/386201384739926/hash_-67270341/hash_-67270341_image_1.jpg)

The scene is from Halt and Catch Fire, AMC’s drama about the 1980s-90s personal computer and internet revolution that aired from 2014 to 2017. AMC used it to promote the premiere of The Audacity, a new darkly comedic tech satire that premiered this month (April 12, 2026) on AMC and AMC+.
The Audacity was created by Jonathan Glatzer (Succession, Better Call Saul). Billy Magnussen plays a flailing data-mining tech CEO navigating power, a mental breakdown, and an unorthodox relationship with his therapist. The cast includes Zach Galifianakis, Sarah Goldberg, Lucy Punch, Simon Helberg, and Rob Corddry.
AMC’s promo framed it as: “Ambition, ego, disruption. Before tonight’s premiere, revisit where the hustle of workplace culture started with Halt and Catch Fire.”
The connection between the 2 shows: both are about tech culture’s capacity to reward the people willing to break the rules. Halt and Catch Fire set that story in the 1980s. The Audacity updates it for Silicon Valley in 2026.
The Audacity screened at SXSW on March 14, 2026 and has already been renewed for Season 2.

![Archive Image](/assets/journal-images/facebook-assets/386201384739926/hash_1525508586/hash_1525508586_image_1.jpg)

29 years later, Event Horizon is finally getting a sequel — and it has the perfect writer
  More in the comments

![Archive Image](/assets/journal-images/facebook-assets/386201384739926/hash_-1188971915/hash_-1188971915_image_1.jpg)

#TheTerror Season 3 is finally coming to AMC+, and we have an exclusive look at the first five minutes of the show. The series was one of the best horror TV shows of the 2010s: Season 1 was a fictionalized account of Captain Sir John Franklin’s real-life expedition to the Arctic in the mid-180s, while Season 2, subtitled Infamy, took place at a U.S. internment camp for Japanese Americans during World War II. Both seasons dealt with scary, supernatural creatures – the first had a terrifying spirit bear known as the Tuunbaq, while the second was the bakemono from Japanese folklore.
The third season, titled Devil in Silver, looks set to continue that in some way. The latest iteration of the anthology show stars Dan Stevens (The Guest, Legion) as Pepper, a working-class man who is wrongfully committed to the New Hyde Psychiatric Hospital. Once there, he uncovers dark secrets, has to combat its patients, and learns of the real terror lurking within its walls. It is based on the novel by Victor LaValle, who serves as a writer and executive producer on the series, alongside Chris Cantwell (who co-created AMC’s Halt and Catch Fire). 
While Stevens’ Pepper isn’t featured in the opening scene, it’s nonetheless a tantalizing hint at what to expect, and suggests the series will be suitably, well, terrifying. It focuses on two staff members at New Hyde having to deal with the dead body of a patient, who has apparently torn himself to death. All we see of that are bloody bedsheets, but the appearance of him underneath is enough to shock the workers. They have to remove him, but his limbs are sticking out and frozen in place, meaning they have to break his bones to fit him through the door, in what’s a gruesome scene that is not for the squeamish.
That’s horrifying enough, but there are some teases that there’s more going on here. The body itself suggests something supernatural could be at play, and it’s even posited that he might not have done it to himself, which begs the question of who, or what, did. Ominously, the camera also lingers on the patient’s room number: 217. That’s the same as the most haunted room at the Overlook Hotel from Stephen King’s The Shining book, which is an Easter egg designed to tell us that some kind of evil spirit lingers there. Given the title, could it even be the devil itself?
It’s all suitably spooky, and very much looks like it’s living up to its predecessors. There’s also no need to fear if you haven’t seen the previous seasons, as the anthology format is designed for this to be a standalone story. The Terror: Devil in Silver premieres on May 7th on AMC+ and Shudder.
Check out the exclusive clip in the comments...

Michael Mann’s genre-defining thriller celebrates its 40th anniversary!
Newly titled MANHUNTER: THE FINAL CUT, the film returns in a stunning 4K restoration, supervised by Michael Mann and debuting on June 12 at The Academy Museum of Motion Pictures. The international premiere follows on June 24 at Cineteca di Bologna, ahead of the US release by Rialto Pictures, in addition to select territories from September.
